articles.title: Tesla hiring over 1,000 workers to ramp up Semi truck production, Business Insider reports
articles.summary: Tesla plans to hire over 1,000 workers in Nevada to increase the mass production of its Semi electric trucks, as reported by Business Insider. The company recently announced that initial production of the Semi trucks will begin at the Gigafactory in Sparks, Nevada, by the end of 2025, aiming for an annual capacity of 50,000 units. This move signifies a significant ramp-up in Tesla's long-delayed truck-making business, which initially aimed for 2019 production.

articles.title: MercadoLibre proposes to move US legal home to Texas from Delaware
articles.summary: MercadoLibre's board has asked shareholders to approve moving its U.S. operations from Delaware to Texas, citing a desire for greater legal certainty. This move, echoing similar actions by companies like Tesla, is scheduled for a shareholder vote on June 17, and if successful, could benefit MercadoLibre while posing a challenge to Asian e-commerce competitors. The company believes Texas's recently enacted rule for codifying business judgments will provide more predictable decision-making for its innovative business model.

articles.title: New PG&E Service Offering Makes It Easier and Faster to Connect EV Chargers, EV Fleets and Big Batteries to the Grid
articles.summary: PG&E has introduced "Flexible Service Connection" (Flex Connect) which uses a cloud-based Distributed Energy Resource Management System (DERMS) to allow EV fast-charging stations and grid-scale batteries to connect to the grid sooner, without waiting for physical infrastructure upgrades. This system coordinates power demand based on electricity supply availability, sending hourly power forecasts to customers. Flex Connect has already expedited connections for Tesla Supercharger sites and PepsiCo's EV semi-truck fleet, enabling earlier EV adoption and significant emissions and cost savings.

articles.title: This Famous Investor Thinks Tesla's Stock Price Will Reach $2,600 Due to 1 Exciting Growth Catalyst
articles.summary: Cathie Wood, manager of the ARK Innovation ETF, believes Tesla's stock price could soar to $2,600 within five years, representing over 1,000% growth from its current $300 per share. Her optimistic forecast is primarily driven by the anticipated rollout of Tesla's robotaxi network and the new Cybercab model in 2026, which she expects to revolutionize autonomous ride-sharing. However, the author expresses skepticism, comparing Wood's predictions to Uber's current market cap and historical over-optimism.

articles.title: Alphabet Sets Positive Tone Ahead of Tech Earnings
articles.summary: This article discusses the Q1 earnings season with a focus on big tech companies. While Tesla reported a decline in earnings and revenue, Alphabet surpassed analyst estimates due to strong performance in its search and advertising units, driven by AI investments. Intel also beat estimates but provided weak forward-looking guidance and announced workforce reductions.

articles.title: TESL Stock Price, News & Analysis
articles.summary: Simplify Volt TSLA Revolution ETF (TESL) stock has seen a 19.9% decline over the past year, with shares last trading at $13.41. The company's short interest has decreased significantly, suggesting limited bearish sentiment, and its low days-to-cover ratio indicates high liquidity. The article provides a comprehensive overview of TESL, including performance data, news, and financial highlights.

articles.title: Elon Musk’s Tesla quietly left off US$97m Bitcoin loss in earnings report
articles.summary: Tesla reportedly excluded a US$97 million Bitcoin-related loss from its adjusted earnings in its latest report, which boosted its non-GAAP earnings by about 12 percent. This accounting practice helped present a stronger financial picture, offsetting what was otherwise Tesla's weakest quarterly performance in years. This inconsistency in how crypto gains and losses are treated in non-GAAP earnings could attract scrutiny from the SEC, which has previously reprimanded companies for similar practices.

articles.title: U.S. Small Vehicle Warranty Expenses:
articles.summary: The U.S. car & cycle industry experienced record highs in warranty claims, accruals, and reserves in 2024, attributed to small recalls, electrical failures, and rising parts costs rather than a single catastrophic event. The report details the warranty expenses of major automakers like Ford, GM, and Tesla, alongside 34 other U.S.-based manufacturers of various small vehicles. It analyzes claims paid, accruals made, and warranty reserve balances, highlighting significant increases for many companies in 2024.
